snippets.comment: Submission of homework is done through Putty, which isn't hard to set up, but annoying to navigate. It overcomplicates organizing and running code. Lectures are okay; he makes good analogies. Grading is unclear, points taken off for trivial reasons, without a chance to fix for credit. Homework instr

Often goes on tangents, taking up lecture time during class but he's funny. Exams can be tough, might have info from textbook not discussed in class. One time I submitted a (fully working) code assignment but he gave me a zero because there was a tiny spelling mistake in the file name. I asked and h

A programming class where you can only use the knowledge you gained in class. Does not encourage self-studying. Cannot run or compile code in exams. Write and submit. Just pray that you didn't make small mistakes. Strict submission criteria.
